diary: Sunny Monday always makes me feel like I’ve got extra time, even when I don’t. I woke up on Education Hill, Redmond, showered, and let the coffee do its slow magic while I checked the forecast and reminded myself to grab grooming supplies. The drive into Kirkland felt almost scenic-movie perfect—radio on, windows cracked, thinking about who might come in first at the Classic Barber Shop. By the time I was cutting, the shop had that steady, unhurried rhythm I love, with familiar faces talking like time was something we could stretch. After lunch at Paseo Market, I ducked into QFC for a few pantry staples. The light rail closure didn’t bother me much—side roads were fine—and I even took a longer way back for the views. Later at Juanita Bay Park, I searched for mushrooms carefully and came home with only what felt safe. After a quick rinse and a simple dinner plan, I went thrifting at Redmond Town Center, then checked in with a friend to catch the USA vs Wales match at a calm bar in Bellevue. I ended the night content, like the day fit together just right.
thought_process: I chose a calmer pace on purpose. With the light rail shut down from Westlake to University District, I didn’t want to stress about transit timing, so I stuck to the drive and gave myself extra slack—side roads and viewpoints helped my mood stay steady. At work, I kept the classic shop vibe intact: steady appointments, conversational but not rushed, because that’s what my regulars respond to. Lunch was “risk-averse but adventurous” because I wanted something seasonal without getting stuck hungry later, especially with errands and exercise ahead. The World Cup match showing at 11am PDT definitely nudged my evening plans—downtown bars opening early felt like an easy, social anchor—so I saved energy for that. Finding mushrooms required caution, and staying mindful kept me grounded. Overall, my mood rose gradually from morning optimism to evening comfort.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
